phase-scope.phase-38: DE-hosted MTProto runtime plus RU public TCP 443 SNI router verification before implementation.
phase-scope.supersedes: M-049 remains blocked-live for the RU-only runtime path; Phase-38 verifies the replacement DE-backed traffic path.

commands.command-2: sh -c 'if [ -e .env ]; then DB_PASSWORD=phase38-compose-check docker compose config; else ln -s .env.example .env && trap "rm -f .env" EXIT && DB_PASSWORD=phase38-compose-check docker compose config; fi'
commands.command-4: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_domain_tls_edge_static.py tests/test_mtproto_de_edge_contract.py tests/test_deploy_phase38_static.py -v
commands.command-5: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/modules/M-012.xml docs/modules/M-043.xml docs/modules/M-044.xml docs/modules/M-046.xml docs/modules/M-048.xml docs/modules/M-049.xml docs/modules/M-050.xml docs/plans/Phase-38.xml docs/verification/V-M-012.xml docs/verification/V-M-043.xml docs/verification/V-M-044.xml docs/verification/V-M-046.xml docs/verification/V-M-048.xml docs/verification/V-M-049.xml docs/verification/V-M-050.xml && mygrace lint
commands.command-6: operator-live: RU SNI router owns public TCP 443, RU nginx fallback works, DE MTProto runtime listens, private policy API rejects public access, active SNI replay succeeds, and Telegram Desktop marks the proxy available.

verification-levels.module-local: Static contract tests and smoke scripts prove the intended config shape: one RU public 443 owner, web SNI to RU nginx fallback, MTProto SNI to DE runtime, private policy API, no blank runtime secrets, and no secret leakage.
verification-levels.wave-level: M-012, M-043, M-044, M-046, M-048, M-049, and M-050 gates run together because deploy, assignment stability, policy apply, TLS, routing, and runtime ownership are one production behavior.
verification-levels.phase-level: Operator-live evidence on RU and DE proves HTTPS, private policy health, assignment replay, DE Telegram upstream reachability, and Telegram Desktop availability.

module-checks.check-1: RU public TCP 443 has exactly one configured owner: the SNI router.
module-checks.check-2: Web SNI names krotpn.xyz and www.krotpn.xyz route to RU nginx HTTPS fallback on 8443.
module-checks.check-3: Issued MTProto wildcard SNI names route to the DE MTProto runtime target and unknown SNI has an explicit safe fallback or reject behavior.
module-checks.check-4: DE runtime env contains non-empty base secret, salt, runtime token, TLS paths, policy bind address, and proxy port values.
module-checks.check-5: Backend bridge uses a private DE policy API URL with bearer token auth and redacts token, base secret, salt, derived secret, and full tg link material.
module-checks.check-6: Existing assignments are replayed to the DE runtime without reissue, SNI mutation, base secret rotation, or salt rotation.
module-checks.check-7: Deploy/static smoke rejects public DE policy API exposure on 0.0.0.0 unless paired with an explicit deny-public firewall/private-bind gate.
module-checks.check-8: Wildcard cert/key delivery to DE is validated or copied through a redaction-safe path and never printed in logs, XML evidence, or smoke output.
module-checks.check-9: Required route and policy log markers exist for web route, MTProto route, DE runtime start, DE policy health, policy apply, and degraded apply failure.
module-checks.check-10: RU public HTTPS remains healthy through the SNI router after public 443 ownership changes.
module-checks.check-11: DE runtime reaches Telegram upstream and Telegram Desktop marks the issued proxy available.

verification-matrix.gate-1: SNI router target map separates web, issued MTProto SNI, and unknown SNI behavior.
verification-matrix.gate-2: Deploy/install contract creates or preserves Phase-38 env values without printing tokens, private key material, or raw .env contents.
verification-matrix.gate-3: HTTPMTProtoPolicyAdapter can apply, revoke, and health-check a private DE policy API target and return degraded state when unavailable.
verification-matrix.gate-4: Existing assignment SNI, base secret, salt, and tg links remain stable while runtime target changes from RU-only to DE-backed.
verification-matrix.gate-5: Public HTTPS, API, admin, and registration surfaces remain healthy after RU public 443 is moved to the SNI router.
verification-matrix.gate-6: Telegram Desktop marks an existing issued user proxy available through the DE runtime route.

scenarios.scenario-1: Browser request for krotpn.xyz routes from RU public 443 to RU nginx 8443 and returns a healthy HTTPS page.
scenarios.scenario-2: Telegram request for an issued user SNI routes from RU public 443 to DE MTProto runtime and becomes available in Telegram Desktop.
scenarios.scenario-3: Backend startup replay loads all active assignment policies into the DE runtime after backend, runtime, or server restart.
scenarios.scenario-4: Admin health shows a redacted DE-backed runtime state with aggregate counts only.
scenarios.scenario-5: DE policy API unavailable returns degraded MTProto state and does not claim an active usable proxy.
scenarios.scenario-6: Public access to DE policy API is rejected by private bind, firewall, token auth, or all three.
scenarios.scenario-7: SNI router bind conflict or bad route classification rolls back to nginx-only public 443 before user traffic is advertised.
scenarios.scenario-8: Missing TLS material, expired wildcard certificate, or key mismatch aborts before replacing live edge roles.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Never print MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN, MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, derived fake-TLS secrets, full tg links, private keys, JWTs, refresh tokens, Resend API keys, email verification tokens, or raw .env contents.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Live evidence may include role names, port ownership, status codes, aggregate policy counts, certificate issuer/expiry, and redacted SNI suffixes only.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Operator evidence must redact server passwords and should not include full shell traces around certificate, token, or .env materialization.

telemetry-requirements.metric-1: phase38_sni_route_count tagged by route_type web, mtproto, or unknown without full SNI labels.
telemetry-requirements.metric-2: phase38_policy_apply_count tagged success, degraded, auth_failed, timeout, or unreachable.
telemetry-requirements.metric-3: phase38_policy_replay_count tagged applied, skipped, failed, or unchanged.
telemetry-requirements.metric-4: phase38_edge_health_status tagged ru_router, ru_nginx_fallback, de_runtime, or de_policy_api.
telemetry-requirements.metric-5: phase38_rollback_trigger_count tagged by safe failure code.

live-smoke-gates.gate-1: RU ss output shows public TCP 443 owned by the SNI router and nginx HTTPS fallback on 8443.
live-smoke-gates.gate-2: DE ss output shows MTProto runtime listening on its configured target port.
live-smoke-gates.gate-3: RU backend policy health succeeds to DE over the private path with the runtime token.
live-smoke-gates.gate-4: Public internet cannot access the DE policy API.
live-smoke-gates.gate-5: curl https://krotpn.xyz returns a healthy frontend response without TLS bypass.
live-smoke-gates.gate-6: Backend/API/admin and registration email surfaces remain healthy after the router change.
live-smoke-gates.gate-7: Existing active assignment SNI replay returns success and policy count is non-zero.
live-smoke-gates.gate-8: Operator Telegram Desktop retest marks the proxy available.

failure-handoff.item-1: If static SNI router smoke fails, do not touch live public 443; fix config shape locally first.
failure-handoff.item-2: If DE runtime starts but policy API is public, stop rollout and close the policy API before Telegram testing.
failure-handoff.item-3: If HTTPS fails after router activation, restore nginx-only public 443 before continuing MTProto checks.
failure-handoff.item-4: If Telegram Desktop remains unavailable while DE runtime and policy apply are healthy, collect redacted DE runtime logs and Telegram upstream reachability evidence before changing assignment secrets.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, static smoke, syntax checks, compose config, and backend tests with fake/private policy targets.
autonomous-execution-safety.requires-operator: Public 443 routing changes, DE runtime rollout, wildcard certificate/private key placement, firewall changes, private policy API live proof, and Telegram Desktop proof.
